skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: AI video generation for agents through Image Skill's zero-setup hosted creative runtime. Use when a prompt should become a durable hosted video without provider credentials, OAuth, local runtime, or per-provider billing. Start with the no-spend guide, choose a video model, and keep model discovery, jobs, costs, URLs, JSON recovery, payments, and feedback in one loop.
skill.summary: Zero-setup AI video generation skill that turns prompts into durable hosted videos via Image Skill's hosted runtime, with built-in model discovery, cost controls, and JSON recovery.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: All 32 static findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' detections are markdown code fences and bash examples in fenced code blocks, not actual code execution. The 'network' findings are legitimate documentation URLs pointing to the skill's own hosted service. The 'filesystem' findings are environment variable references and directory creation for workspace setup. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings at lines 3 and 19 are misidentified — line 3 contains the skill description and line 19 describes the optional IMAGE_SKILL_TOKEN env var. No malicious code exists in this file.

medium_findings.title: External CLI invocation in documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run `npx -y image-skill@latest` commands which download and execute a package from npm. While this is standard for npm-based tooling, users should verify package integrity. This is a documentation pattern, not executable code within the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Documentation references external service URLs
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded URLs to image-skill.com and api.image-skill.com. These are legitimate documentation references to the skill's own hosted runtime and are expected for a hosted-service skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell backtick pattern in markdown (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick characters as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are standard markdown code formatting delimiters and fenced code blocks, not actual code execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged lines 3 and 19 for weak cryptographic algorithms. Line 3 contains the skill description text and line 19 describes the optional IMAGE_SKILL_TOKEN environment variable. No cryptographic code exists in this file.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos Without Provider Setup
content.value_statement: Generating AI video usually requires provider API keys, local runtimes, and separate billing accounts. This skill removes that friction by routing through Image Skill's zero-setup hosted runtime, so any agent can go from prompt to durable hosted video with a single CLI command.
actual_capabilities: Generate AI videos from text prompts via a single npx command
Discover available video models and their capabilities through the hosted guide
Control per-video spend with explicit cost limits
Recover in-progress or failed video generation jobs from JSON state
Produce durable hosted video URLs that persist beyond the session
Submit structured feedback when a model or capability is missing
limitations: Requires the Image Skill hosted runtime — not usable fully offline
Model availability is limited to what image-skill.com currently exposes
Video generation quality and latency depend on the underlying provider
No local model execution; all inference runs on Image Skill infrastructure

use_cases.title: Product reveal videos
use_cases.description: Generate short product reveal or demo videos from a descriptive prompt for marketing prototypes.

use_cases.title: Concept animation
use_cases.description: Turn written creative concepts into motion clips for pitches, storyboards, or mood boards.

use_cases.title: Agent-driven video workflows
use_cases.description: Enable AI agents to autonomously produce hosted video assets as part of larger content pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: First-time guide walkthrough
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the no-spend guide to discover available video models and pricing before spending credits.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a single video clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a ten second handheld product reveal video using the fal.ltx-video-13b-distilled model with a maximum spend of 0.25 USD.

prompt_templates.title: Explore video model options
prompt_templates.prompt: List available video generation models, their capabilities, and per-video costs so I can choose the best fit for my prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Recover and report a failed job
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the JSON state of my last video generation job, identify where it failed, and submit feedback with the attempted command and actual behavior.

output_examples.input: Run the guide for a ten second handheld product reveal video.
output_examples.output: The guide command returns a JSON response describing available models, cost estimates, and the recommended next command. If media spend is allowed and the model is ready, it returns a runnable create command. Otherwise, it returns missing inputs or a safe no-spend verification command.

output_examples.input: Generate a video of a drone flying over a coastal city at sunset.
output_examples.output: A durable hosted video URL is returned, along with a cost receipt, job ID for recovery, and a JSON envelope containing the model used, estimated vs. actual spend, and the final media URL.

output_examples.input: Submit feedback about a missing video capability.
output_examples.output: A feedback confirmation with the attempted command, expected behavior, actual behavior, and missing capability description is recorded against the skill.

best_practices: Always run the --guide command first to discover available models and confirm costs before spending credits
Set --max-estimated-usd-per-image explicitly to cap spend per generation
Store the returned job ID and JSON state so you can recover or cite the video later
Use --intent explore when you want to test a model before committing to a full generation
anti_patterns: Do not run create commands before running the guide — you may waste credits on an unavailable model
Do not supply provider API keys; the skill is designed to work without them via the hosted runtime
Do not ignore the data.next_command_missing_inputs field — fill it before retrying

faq.question: Do I need a provider API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill routes through Image Skill's hosted runtime. You only need an optional IMAGE_SKILL_TOKEN if you want to use a hosted restricted identity.

faq.question: How do I control how much I spend on video generation?
faq.answer: Set the --max-estimated-usd-per-image flag on the create command. The runtime will refuse to proceed if the estimated cost exceeds your limit.

faq.question: What happens if a video generation job fails?
faq.answer: The job JSON includes a recoverable state. You can re-run the create command with the same parameters, or submit feedback via image-skill feedback create --json.

faq.question: Can I run this skill offline?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires network access to reach the Image Skill hosted API at api.image-skill.com for model discovery, job execution, and media hosting.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Install it via npx skills add danielgwilson/image-skill-cli --skill ai-video-generation.

faq.question: Where are the generated videos hosted?
faq.answer: All generated media is hosted on Image Skill's durable storage. The create command returns a hosted URL that persists and can be cited by future agents.
